diagrams.title: Row Echelon Form Structure
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a matrix in row echelon form showing the characteristic staircase pattern with highlighted pivot positions. The diagram displays a 4x5 matrix with leading 1s in positions (1,1), (2,2), (3,3), and (4,4), demonstrating how each pivot appears strictly to the right of the pivot above it.
elements: Pivot positions marked with colored circles
Zero entries below each pivot shown in lighter shade
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the defining structure of row echelon form - the staircase pattern created by pivots - which is fundamental to understanding rank and determinant calculations.

diagrams.title: Matrix Rank Comparison
diagrams.description: Three side-by-side matrices of the same dimensions (3x4) demonstrating different ranks: rank 3 (full rank), rank 2, and rank 1. Each matrix shows pivot positions with color-coding, and a bar chart visual below each showing the rank value, helping students grasp how rank equals the number of non-zero rows in echelon form.
elements: Pivot positions highlighted in distinct colors
Zero rows indicated with dashed outline
Rank counter display for each matrix
Visual bar representation of rank values
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates that rank is the count of non-zero rows in echelon form, providing intuitive visual comparison between matrices of varying ranks from the same dimensional space.

diagrams.title: Determinant from Echelon Form
diagrams.description: A step-by-step transformation diagram showing a 3x3 matrix being converted to upper triangular form via elementary row operations. Each operation box shows the operation applied, its effect on the determinant (multiply by scalar, add multiple, swap rows), and the running determinant calculation. The final result shows det = product of diagonal elements.
elements: Determinant change indicators at each step
Final upper triangular matrix with diagonal highlighted
Determinant calculation: det = d₁ × d₂ × d₃
diagrams.purpose: Shows the computational connection: since row operations transform the matrix to triangular form while tracking determinant changes, the determinant equals the product of diagonal entries in echelon/triangular form.
